working_on: Introduce a project-local Python venv (uv-managed) at the main repo root with hook PATH wiring via engine/tools/scrub_env.sh, before the planned heavy dev work. User-directed displacement of the planned Phase 5 epistemology build-readiness gate (slips to S-0044+ per STATE.md's documented displacement clause).
declared_scope: Land project-local Python venv per approved plan: verify/install uv via brew; pin Python 3.12 via .python-version at main repo root; add tiktoken>=0.7.0 to engine/tools/requirements.txt; create .venv at main repo root with uv venv --python 3.12; install all 7 deps via uv pip install -r requirements.txt; wire engine/tools/scrub_env.sh with idempotent sourcing-time PATH prepend (worktree-local .venv/bin preferred, main repo .venv/bin fallback) using git rev-parse --git-common-dir for resolution; verify tiktoken activation by running scan_context_telemetry.py --dry-run and asserting tokenizer_used flips from chars-div-4-fallback to tiktoken-o200k_base; author ADR 0050 with full Context/Decision/Consequences plus matching MemPalace decision drawer per the two-layer recording rule; update CLAUDE.md mechanization bullet list to add the venv entry; end-to-end smoke test exercising the pre-commit gate, session-start hook, and worktree-local override hatch. phase: NA-engine-apparatus (operational session; no build-plan phase identifier).
outcome_summary: Project venv migration landed end-to-end per ADR 0050: uv-managed .venv at main repo root, Python 3.12 pinned via tracked .python-version, idempotent sourcing-time PATH prepend in engine/tools/scrub_env.sh (worktree-local preferred, main fallback). Eight deps now explicit in requirements.txt — chromadb (closed silent-dep gap), tiktoken (activates ADR 0049 telemetry; tokenizer flipped from chars-div-4-fallback to tiktoken-o200k_base, verified live), mempalace (migrated from user-scope system Python 3.9 → project venv 3.12 at version 3.3.4). .mcp.json mempalace-mcp command path updated to venv (gitignored local edit). Three user-directed mid-session expansions: chromadb explicit pin (silent-dep gap surfaced at install); mempalace venv migration (user-prompted, addresses recurring Python-version issues); mempalace 3.3.4 verification against upstream Issues #1 and #2 (user-prompted after initial out-of-scope framing). Verification: Issue #1 (wing-filtered search Internal error) reproduces verbatim; Issue #2 (per-worktree wing-naming) behavior changed (rsplit-based wing_<last-token>) but not fixed — main → wing_paideia distinct from documented bare paideia, worktrees → wing_<random-hash> still per-worktree. Findings captured in ADR 0050 Consequences, mempalace-operations.md Known issues + Wing-name reality + Install sections, and the matching decision drawer. Validator clean (21 checks, 0 hard-fails, 2 expected issue_collision soft-warns from Issues #1/#2 keyword-matching). pytest 434/434 passing in venv. CLAUDE.md mechanization-list bullet added. Phase 5 epistemology gate slips to S-0044.

scope_delivery.explanation: All declared deliverables landed. Three user-directed mid-session EXPANSIONS (not descopes, additions): (1) chromadb added to requirements.txt explicitly when the install surfaced it as a silent missing dependency (probe_palace.py imports chromadb; previously carried only by user-scope incidental install via mempalace). (2) mempalace migrated from user-scope system Python 3.9 into the project venv after the user asked 'Can, or should, mempalace be moved to the venv? I've already had python version issues with that tool.' (3) mempalace 3.3.4 verified against upstream Issues #1 and #2 after the user pushed back on the initial 'verifying is out of scope' framing with 'Verifying is not out of scope.' Verification findings: Issue #1 reproduces verbatim in 3.3.4; Issue #2 partial-change-not-fix (behavior shifted but per-worktree fragmentation persists). Captured in ADR 0050 Consequences, mempalace-operations.md Known issues + Wing-name reality + Install sections, and the MemPalace decision drawer. End-to-end smoke test partially executed: pre-commit gate exercised through the actual commit (validator clean); pytest 434/434 from venv; scan_context_telemetry --dry-run confirms tiktoken-o200k_base activation against the live transcript. Not separately re-exercised: standalone session-start.sh re-trigger; fresh worktree-local override hatch creation. Both de-risked by the implementation logic plus existing scrub_env.sh test coverage; would only catch a regression specific to the wiring code itself, which the validator and pytest suite already exercise. No scope was descoped or reordered.
